description: Time-limited rotating menu announcements highlighting new weekly dessert items with specific date ranges and visual emphasis on novelty and exclusivity.
structural_recipe: Lead with emoji-heavy excitement markers (🔥🔥 or 🤎) followed by date range in MM-DD format. Structure: [HEADLINE] → [DATE RANGE] → [SECTION HEADER: WEEKLY ROTATING MENU] → [Item name in caps] → [Item description with sensory details]. Use multiple emojis strategically throughout. Emphasize NEW items and ingredient combinations. Include visual separators (emoji borders). Layer flavor stories (e.g., S'mores + Oreo pairing) to create narrative cohesion.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences respond to scarcity and time-limitation psychology (FOMO). Weekly rotation creates predictable check-in behavior and repeat visits. Emoji-dense captions feel festive and appeal to younger demographics. Sensory descriptors (gooey, soft, golden) trigger appetite appeal. Novelty positioning (NEW, first appearance) drives curiosity and trial among followers.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure date ranges respect Islamic calendar events (Ramadan, Eid) when promoting indulgent desserts
Verify alcohol-free ingredients for S'mores/chocolate items to comply with local norms
Emoji density works for youth-targeted brands but may underperform with older demographics
cultural_constraints: Avoid promotion during fasting hours in Ramadan
Ensure halal certification visible or mentioned for food items
Weekend-weighted promotions (Thu-Sat) align with Saudi leisure patterns
